name: math_e
title: math_e
description:
The Number value for e, Euler's number,
which is approximately 2.718281828459045.

name: math_inf
title: math_inf
description: The name inf refers to float value positive infinity. (For negative infinity, use -math.inf.) Equivalent to the output of float('inf'). See also Python 3.13 Documentation.

name: math_nan
title: math_nan
description: A floating-point “not a number” (nan) value. Equivalent to the output of float('nan'). See also Python 3.13 Documentation.

name: math_pi
title: math_pi
description: The float value of π, the ratio of the circumference of a circle to its diameter, which is approximately 3.1415926535897932.

name: math_tau
title: math_tau
description: Tau is a circle constant equals to 2π, the ratio of a circle’s circumference to its radius, which is approximately 6.283185307179586.

name: math_fabs
title: math_fabs(x) -> float
description: Return the absolute value of a number as a float. Unlike the built-in abs(), math_fabs() always returns a float, even when the input is an integer. It only accepts int or float types (complex numbers are not supported).

name: math_comb
title: math_comb(n, k) -> int
description: Return the number of ways to choose k items from n items without repetition and without order. Returns zero when k > n.

name: math_factorial
title: math_factorial(n) -> int
description: Return n factorial as an integer.

name: math_gcd
title: math_gcd(*integers) -> int
description: Return the greatest common divisor of the specified *integers arguments. If any of the arguments is nonzero, then the returned value is the largest positive integer that is a divisor of all arguments. If all arguments are 0, then the returned value is 0. gcd() without arguments returns 0. If any of the provided integers is negative, the function treats it as its absolute value when computing the GCD.

name: math_isqrt
title: math_isqrt(n) -> int
description: Return the integer square root of the non-negative n.

name: math_lcm
title: math_lcm(*integers) -> int
description: Return the least common multiple of the specified integer arguments. If all arguments are nonzero, then the returned value is the smallest positive integer that is a multiple of all arguments. If any of the arguments is 0, then the returned value is 0. lcm() without arguments returns 1. If any of the input integers is negative, math_lcm() treats it as its absolute value when computing the LCM, so the result is always non-negative.

name: math_perm
title: math_perm(n, k) -> int
description: Return the number of ways to choose k items from n items without repetition and with order. Returns zero when k > n.

name: math_ceil
title: math_ceil(x) -> int
description: Return the ceiling of x, the smallest integer greater than or equal to x.

name: math_floor
title: math_floor(x) -> int
description: Return the floor of x, the largest integer less than or equal to x.

name: math_fma
title: math_fma(x, y, z) -> float
description: Fused multiply–add operation. Return (x * y) + z, computed as though with infinite precision and range followed by a single round to the float format. This operation often provides better accuracy than the direct expression (x * y) + z. This function follows the specification of the (fusedMultiplyAdd) operation described in the IEEE 754 standard. The standard leaves one case implementation-defined, namely the result of fma(0, inf, nan) and fma(inf, 0, nan). In these cases, math.fma returns a math.nan, and does not raise any exception.

name: math_fmod
title: math_fmod(x, y) -> float
description: Return the floating-point remainder of x / y, as defined by the platform C library function fmod(x, y). The sign of the result is the same as the sign of x.

name: math_remainder
title: math_remainder(x, y) -> float
description: Return the IEEE 754-style remainder of x with respect to y. For finite x and finite nonzero y, this is the difference x - n*y, where n is the closest integer to the exact value of the quotient x / y. If x / y is exactly halfway between two consecutive integers, the nearest even integer is used for n. The remainder r = remainder(x, y) thus always satisfies abs(r) <= 0.5 * abs(y).

name: math_trunc
title: math_trunc(x) -> int
description: Return x with the fractional part removed, leaving the integer part. trunc() is equivalent to floor() for positive x, and equivalent to ceil() for negative x.

name: math_copysign
title: math_copysign(x, y) -> float
description: Return a float with the magnitude (absolute value) of x but the sign of y.

name: math_isfinite
title: math_isfinite(x) -> bool
description: Return True if x is neither an infinity nor a nan, and False otherwise.

name: math_isinf
title: math_isinf(x) -> bool
description: Return True if x is a positive or negative infinity, and False otherwise.

name: math_isnan
title: math_isnan(x) -> bool
description: Return True if x is a nan (not a number), and False otherwise.

name: math_ldexp
title: math_ldexp(x, i) -> float
description: Return x * (2**i). This is essentially the inverse of function frexp().

name: math_nextafter
title: math_nextafter(x, y, steps) -> float
description: Return the floating-point value steps steps after x towards y. If x is equal to y, return y, unless steps is 0.

name: math_ulp
title: math_ulp(x) -> float
description: Return the value of the least significant bit of the float x. If x is a NaN (not a number), return x. If x is negative, return ulp(-x). If x is a positive infinity, return x. If x is equal to 0, return the smallest positive denormalized representable float (smaller than the minimum positive normalized float, sys.float_info.min, approximately 1.7976931348623157e+308). If x is equal to the largest positive representable float, return the value of the least significant bit of x, such that the first float smaller than x is x - ulp(x). Otherwise (when x is a positive finite number), return the value of the least significant bit of x, such that the first float bigger than x is x + ulp(x).

name: math_cbrt
title: math_cbrt(x) -> float
description: Return the cube root of x.

name: math_exp
title: math_exp(x) -> float
description: Return e raised to the power x, where e = 2.718281… is the base of natural logarithms.

name: math_exp2
title: math_exp2(x) -> float
description: Return 2 raised to the power x.

name: math_expm1
title: math_expm1(x) -> float
description: Return e raised to the power x, minus 1. Here e is the base of natural logarithms. For small x, the subtraction in exp(x) - 1 can result in a significant loss of precision; the expm1() function provides a way to compute this quantity to full precision.

name: math_log
title: math_log(x, base(optional)) -> float
description: With one argument, return the natural logarithm of x (to base e). With two arguments, return the logarithm of x to the given base, calculated as log(x)/log(base).

name: math_log1p
title: math_log1p(x) -> float
description: Return the natural logarithm of 1+x (base e). The result is calculated in a way which is accurate for x near 0.

name: math_log2
title: math_log2(x) -> float
description: Return the base-2 logarithm of x. This is usually more accurate than log(x, 2).

name: math_log10
title: math_log10(x) -> float
description: Return the base-10 logarithm of x. This is usually more accurate than log(x, 10).

name: math_pow
title: math_pow(x, y) -> float
description: Return x raised to the power y. Unlike the built-in ** operator, math_pow() converts both its arguments to type float.

name: math_sqrt
title: math_sqrt(x) -> float
description: Return the square root of x.

name: math_degrees
title: math_degrees(x) -> float
description: Convert angle x from radians to degrees.

name: math_radians
title: math_radians(x) -> float
description: Convert angle x from degrees to radians.

name: math_acos
title: math_acos(x) -> float
description: Return the arc cosine of x, in radians. The result is between 0 and pi.

name: math_asin
title: math_asin(x) -> float
description: Return the arc sine of x, in radians. The result is between -pi/2 and pi/2.

name: math_atan
title: math_atan(x) -> float
description: Return the arc tangent of x, in radians. The result is between -pi/2 and pi/2.

name: math_atan2
title: math_atan2(y, x) -> float
description: Return atan(y / x), in radians.

name: math_cos
title: math_cos(x) -> float
description: Return the cosine of x radians.

name: math_sin
title: math_sin(x) -> float
description: Return the sine of x radians.

name: math_tan
title: math_tan(x) -> float
description: Return the tangent of x radians.

name: math_acosh
title: math_acosh(x) -> float
description: Return the inverse hyperbolic cosine of x.

name: math_asinh
title: math_asinh(x) -> float
description: Return the inverse hyperbolic sine of x.

name: math_atanh
title: math_atanh(x) -> float
description: Return the inverse hyperbolic tangent of x.

name: math_cosh
title: math_cosh(x) -> float
description: Return the hyperbolic cosine of x.

name: math_sinh
title: math_sinh(x) -> float
description: Return the hyperbolic sine of x.

name: math_tanh
title: math_tanh(x) -> float
description: Return the hyperbolic tangent of x.

name: math_erf
title: math_erf(x) -> float
description: Return the error function at x.

name: math_erfc
title: math_erfc(x) -> float
description: Return the complementary error function at x. The complementary error function is defined as 1.0 - erf(x). It is used for large values of x where a subtraction from one would cause a loss of significance.

name: math_gamma
title: math_gamma(x) -> float
description: Return the Gamma function at x.

name: math_lgamma
title: math_lgamma(x) -> float
description: Return the natural logarithm of the absolute value of the Gamma function at x.
